data.title: Harmony Biosciences reports Q3 revenue of $239M, raises FY25 revenue guidance
data.description:
• Harmony Biosciences (NASDAQ:HRMY) on Thursday reported net revenue of ~$239M in Q3 2025, vs consensus of $220.04M, representing 29% year-over-year revenue growth.
  • These results were driven by the highest ever quarterly increase in average number of patients of about 500 for the quarter, reaching 8,100 average patients in Q3 2025, the company said.
  • Harmony increased its 2025 revenue guidance to $845-$865 million from the previous range of $820-$860 million, vs consensus of $841.56M.
  • The company will report full Q3 2025 earnings and host a conference call on November 4th.
  • HRMY +6.59% premarket to $28.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: VSee Health eliminates $5M debt
data.description:
• VSee Health (NASDAQ:VSEE) said on Thursday that it has eliminated over $5M in legacy SPAC debt.
  • VSEE +4.36% premarket to $0.7252.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dover rises after quarterly earnings beat estimates
data.description:
Dover (NYSE:DOV) rose 1.5% in premarket trading Thursday after the industrial manufacturer reported third-quarter results that beat Wall Street’s profit expectations but slightly missed on revenue.

The company posted adjusted earnings of $2.62 a share, ahead of analysts’ forecasts for $2.51 a share. Revenue rose 5% from a year earlier to $2.08 billion, just shy of the $2.11 billion expected by analysts.

Net income from continuing operations fell to $303 million, or $2.20 a share, from $313 million, or $2.26 a share, a year earlier, in line with Wall Street’s estimate for GAAP earnings of $2.20 a share.

Adjusted earnings from continuing operations climbed 15% to $361 million, driven by broad-based shipment growth in short-cycle components, strong performance in secular-growth markets and contributions from recent acquisitions, the company said.

“We are pleased with Dover’s third quarter results,” President and Chief Executive Richard Tobin said in a statement. “Margin performance in the quarter was exemplary, with a record consolidated segment margin, a result of positive mix impact, solid execution, and our rigorous cost containment and productivity actions.”

data.title: Boeing names former White House aide to lead in key Chinese market
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) appointed Landon Loomis as its new president for China, selecting a fluent Mandarin speaker with both aviation and government experience to help navigate a turbulent period in U.S.-China trade relations.

Loomis, who previously worked at the U.S. Embassy in Beijing as an aviation specialist and later served as a special adviser to former Vice President Mike Pence, will also retain his role as Boeing’s (NYSE:BA) vice president of global policy. Based in Beijing, he will oversee the company’s operations, strategic planning, and engagement with Chinese authorities.

The leadership change comes amid renewed tensions between Washington and Beijing, with Boeing (BA) caught in the middle of tariff disputes and export restrictions. Earlier this year, China temporarily paused deliveries of new Boeing (BA) aircraft before resuming them. U.S. officials have also hinted that exports of aircraft parts to China could face future restrictions.

“Landon is the ideal leader for Boeing China,” said Brendan Nelson, president of Boeing Global. “His deep industry and government experience, combined with years of living and working in China, will further strengthen our long-term partnerships and presence here.”

Boeing (BA) is said to be in talks to sell as many as 500 planes to Chinese airlines, a potential lifeline for the manufacturer, dependent on improved political and trade conditions. Meanwhile, rival Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) expanded its footprint in China this week by launching a second A320neo assembly line in Tianjin, intensifying competition in one of the world’s most important aviation markets.

data.title: World Kinect appoints Ira M. Birns as CEO
data.description:
• World Kinect Corporation (NYSE:WKC) announced on Thursday a series of executive leadership appointments. Effective January 1, 2026, the board has appointed Ira M. Birns as Chief Executive Officer and as a member of the board. 
  • Birns succeeds Michael Kasbar, the company’s chairman and CEO. Kasbar will transition to Executive Chairman of the board, effective January 1, 2026.
  • Also effective January 1, 2026, John Rau, currently Chief Operating Officer, has been promoted to President and will continue to oversee the company’s global commercial operations.
  • Mike Tejada, currently Senior Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer, is being promoted to Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer effective immediately.

data.title: IBM's slide on Red Hat slowdown just be 'knee-jerk weakness,' analysts believe
data.description:
Although IBM's (NYSE:IBM) results and guidance were better-than-expected, some concerns about the software business may have spooked investors, Wall Street analysts said.

Shares fell 6.9% in premarket trading. 

IBM said revenue for its hybrid cloud unit, which is largely made up of Red Hat, rose 14% or 12% in constant currency, below what analysts had expected. 

Stifel analyst David Grossman was positive on the overall results, but cautioned that the “software reset” could hurt the stock in the near-term. Given that shares were up 18% over the past seven weeks, there was a “difficult set-up” going into earnings and Grossman said it was “not surprising” that the stock was re-rating down.

“Fundamentally, business is solid and we reiterate our Buy; however, defensive-minded investors may experience some short-term softness,” Grossman wrote in a note to clients.

Wedbush Securities was also positive on the results and pointed to the $9.5B backlog in IBM's generative AI book of business as an indicator that AI-related projects are ramping “big.” 

“We continue to believe IBM is well-positioned to capitalize on the current demand wave for hybrid cloud and AI applications as more enterprises are looking to leverage these capabilities across organizational workflows,” Wedbush analyst Dan Ives wrote in a note to clients. “We would be buyers of any modest knee-jerk weakness in the stock.” Wedbush has an Outperform rating and $325 price target on IBM. 

Bank of America analyst Wamsi Mohan slightly lowered his price target to $310 from $315 after the results, and said the company is seeing the “right growth,” albeit the “wrong mix.”

“Despite the disappointing mix of SW growth, IBM raised F25 guidance for total rev growth in cc to more than 5%, PTI margin expansion of more than 1%, and FCF to ~$14.0bn,” Mohan wrote in a note to clients. “For RHT, [management] acknowledged the deceleration q/q (execution challenges and below model consumption services) and revised their F25 guidance to the “low end of mid-teens” y/y growth. Reit. Buy as IBM is mixing up to higher margin Software and driving strong FCF and we expect continued positive estimate revisions.”

data.title: Millrose Properties FFO of $0.74 beats by $0.05, revenue of $179.3M beats by $1.74M
data.description:
• Millrose Properties press release (NYSE:MRP): Q3 FFO of $0.74 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $179.3M beats by $1.74M.
  • Raising guidance for year-end Adjusted Funds from Operations (AFFO) quarterly run rate to a range of $0.74 to $0.76 per share.
  • Increasing target for full-year 2025 new transaction funding under Other Agreements to $2.2 billion, driven by continued strong demand for Millrose’s homesite option platform.
